skill.name: pexoai-agent
skill.description: Create professional short videos (5-120 seconds) using AI. Upload assets, send creative requests, and receive finished videos with aspect ratios for social media platforms.
skill.summary: AI-powered video creation agent that produces short videos for social media, product ads, and brand content.
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: This skill is an API client for the Pexo AI video platform. Static analysis detected 534 potential security patterns across 13 files, but the majority are false positives reflecting standard CLI tool behavior. The skill uses shell scripts to make authenticated HTTP requests, read local configuration, and manage temporary files. Detected patterns (external commands, network calls, filesystem access, environment variable reads) are all expected for an API client skill. Weak cryptographic algorithms (MD5/SHA1) are used for file checksums, which is low-risk in this context. No credential exfiltration, unauthorized network access, or malicious patterns were found after manual evaluation.

high_findings.title: API Key Access from Local Configuration
high_findings.description: The skill reads PEXO_API_KEY from a local config file (~/.pexo/config) and uses it in HTTP Authorization headers. This is expected behavior for an API client skill, but grants the skill access to the user's API credentials. If the skill were modified maliciously, it could exfiltrate the key.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern confirmed: PEXO_API_KEY is sourced from config and used in Authorization header. This is legitimate API client behavior, but flagged as high-risk because the skill has access to credentials.

high_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Usage
high_findings.description: Multiple scripts use MD5 or SHA1 for file checksums and hashing. These algorithms are cryptographically weak and should not be used for security-critical operations. In this context, they are used for file integrity checks and deduplication, not security.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern confirmed: md5sum/sha1sum commands used in scripts for file hashing. Not security-critical in this context, but flagged as weak crypto.

medium_findings.title: External Shell Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill uses extensive shell command substitution ($(command)) and curl invocations to interact with the Pexo API. This is legitimate CLI tooling for an API client, but the pattern triggers static analysis due to the general risk of command injection if user input were passed unsafely to shell commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern confirmed: shell commands are used for legitimate API calls and file operations. No user-input injection vectors found in the script source, but SKILL.md instructs the AI agent to execute commands which requires trust in the AI's behavior.

medium_findings.title: Network Access to External API
medium_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP requests to pexo.ai and related URLs. This is expected for an API client, but the network access could be misused if the skill were modified to send data to unauthorized endpoints.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern confirmed: curl commands target pexo.ai API endpoints. All URLs point to the official Pexo service, which is consistent with the skill's stated purpose.

medium_findings.title: Hidden File Access in Home Directory
medium_findings.description: The skill reads from ~/.pexo/config and writes to ~/.pexo/tmp. This is standard configuration and temporary file storage for a CLI tool, but access to hidden files in the home directory could be a vector for reading sensitive data if misused.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern confirmed: config sourced from ~/.pexo/config and temp directory at ~/.pexo/tmp. These are the skill's own files, not unrelated hidden files.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs in Documentation
low_findings.description: The SKILL.md and reference files contain hardcoded URLs to pexo.ai documentation and API endpoints. These are legitimate service URLs, not malicious endpoints.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern confirmed: all URLs point to pexo.ai or pexo-skills GitHub repository. These are legitimate documentation and API references.

low_findings.title: System Information Commands in Diagnostic Scripts
low_findings.description: The doctor and troubleshooting scripts use commands like uname, df, and free to diagnose system configuration issues. These are legitimate diagnostic tools for a setup wizard.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern confirmed: system diagnostic commands used in doctor/troubleshooting scripts for legitimate setup assistance purposes.

content.value_statement: This skill connects your AI coding agent to Pexo, a professional video creation platform. It lets you upload assets, send creative requests, and receive finished short videos without leaving your AI workflow.
actual_capabilities: Upload images, audio, and video files to the Pexo platform via automated scripts
Send creative requests to Pexo's AI video agent and receive finished videos
Manage video projects including creation, progress polling, and listing
Support multiple output aspect ratios (16:9, 9:16, 1:1) for different platforms
Generate videos between 5 and 120 seconds in length
Handle error states including credit balance checks and setup diagnostics
limitations: Requires a Pexo API key and account with available credits
Video generation is asynchronous and requires polling for completion
Depends on the Pexo backend service availability and API rate limits

use_cases.title: Social Media Content Creation
use_cases.description: Marketing teams and content creators can generate short videos for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts directly from their AI coding workflow.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media managers

use_cases.title: Product Video Ads
use_cases.description: E-commerce businesses and startups can create product demonstration videos and promotional clips by uploading product images and describing the desired output.

use_cases.title: Explainer Video Production
use_cases.description: Educators, trainers, and technical writers can produce short explainer videos by describing concepts and requesting visual demonstrations from the AI video agent.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Video Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 15-second vertical video about morning coffee for Instagram Stories.
prompt_templates.scenario: A beginner wants a quick social media video without complex requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Product Video with Asset Upload
prompt_templates.prompt: I want a 30-second product video for my running shoes. I am uploading the product photo.
prompt_templates.scenario: An e-commerce seller creates a promotional video using a product image.

prompt_templates.title: Brand Video with Style Direction
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a 60-second brand video for our fitness app. Use an energetic, modern style with upbeat pacing. Target audience is young professionals.
prompt_templates.scenario: A marketing team member creates a brand video with specific style guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Asset Explainer Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an explainer video about how our API works. I am uploading our logo, a screenshot of the dashboard, and an architecture diagram. Use these as visual references.
prompt_templates.scenario: A developer creates a technical explainer video using multiple reference assets.

output_examples.input: Create a 10-second video of a sunset over the ocean.
I need a product video for my coffee mug. I am uploading the product photo.
output: Video request submitted with product image reference
Status: Processing - you will receive the video link when ready

best_practices: Provide clear, specific descriptions of the video you want. The more detail you give, the better the output.
Upload relevant reference images before making your request to guide the AI video agent.
Use the project polling feature to check progress. Videos take 2-5 minutes to generate on average.
anti_patterns: Do not add technical parameters like duration or aspect ratio unless the user specifically requests them. The AI video agent knows which settings to use.
Do not modify the user's original message when relaying it to the video agent. Add only asset tags for uploaded files.
Do not attempt to use this skill without configuring your Pexo API key. Run the setup diagnostics first if you encounter authentication errors.

faq.question: What video formats does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill creates short videos between 5 and 120 seconds. Supported aspect ratios are 16:9 (landscape), 9:16 (portrait), and 1:1 (square).

faq.question: How long does video generation take?
faq.answer: Videos typically take 2-5 minutes to generate. The system processes requests asynchronously, so you submit your request and poll for the result.

faq.question: Do I need a Pexo account?
faq.answer: Yes. You need a Pexo account with a valid API key and available credits. Run the setup script to configure your credentials before using the skill.

faq.question: Can I upload images or other files?
faq.answer: Yes. You can upload images, audio, and video files using the upload script. These assets can be referenced in your video creation requests.

faq.question: What happens if my video request fails?
faq.answer: The skill provides error messages and diagnostic information. Common issues include invalid API keys, insufficient credits, or project incompatibility. Run the doctor script for troubleshooting.

faq.question: Which AI platforms support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It integrates as an AI skill that your coding agent can invoke when video creation is needed.
